release.description: The core Nightforge workflow for Midnight development — command guides, troubleshooting, and contributor guidance.
bullets: Core toolkit commands: init, compile, wallet, proof-server, deploy, clean
Interactive selection for compile entry-files and deploy artifacts

items.q: Does Nightforge replace the Compact compiler?
items.a: No. Nightforge orchestrates workflows and calls Compact compiler as part of the pipeline. It's a toolkit layer on top of existing Midnight infrastructure.

items.q: How are multiple contract files handled?
items.a: The compile command prompts for entry-file selection when multiple .compact files exist, or you can pass the file path explicitly.

items.q: How are multiple deploy artifacts handled?
items.a: Deploy prompts for artifact selection if multiple compiled artifacts exist. Pass the name explicitly to skip the prompt.

items.q: Do I need proof-server running before deploy?
items.a: Yes, standard deploy requires proof-server readiness. Use --auto flag to let Nightforge handle the full orchestration automatically.

items.q: Can I automate the entire deploy flow?
items.a: Yes! Use nightforge deploy --auto for end-to-end orchestration — it handles proof-server startup, compilation checks, and deployment in sequence.

finalCta.subtitle: From project scaffold to deployed contract in minutes, not hours.
